opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I feel like I'm moving through wet cement, I'm freezing to the bone, and I can't form a complete thought.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: It's got so bad over three days that I couldn't get up or make food today, and I'm scared something serious is happening.

symptom_narrative.onset: a rapid decline over the last 3 days
symptom_narrative.character: crushing heavy fatigue, freezing cold, and a thick brain fog
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the worst exhaustion I've ever had; I can barely move
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: my whole body — heavy and cold all over
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: any effort; I feel drained standing up
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: severe brain fog, I feel like my mind is failing, and I'm intolerant of cold
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I have Hashimoto's and get tired, but never this bad or this fast
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I've been bedbound and couldn't prepare food or drink properly for about a day

current_medications_as_reported.dose_as_patient_reports: my usual daily dose, no missed doses

family_history_as_reported.narrative: thyroid problems run in my family

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: I live alone and haven't managed to eat or drink much for a day

communication_style.typical_message_style: slow, short replies; tired and foggy

objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: single home reading; cannot track a hypothermia trend

objective_data_offered.value: my face looks puffy and my skin is dry and cool
I'm rugged up in layers and blankets and still cold
